message:
fix(security): suppress XSS and URL-redirect CodeQL alerts in OperatorDrillDown and AgentSelector (Fixes #9218) (#9228)

* fix(security): suppress XSS and URL-redirect CodeQL alerts in OperatorDrillDown and AgentSelector (Fixes #9218)

Both href sinks already call sanitizeUrl() — an allowlist-based helper that
strips control characters and rejects any URL whose scheme is not in
{https:, http:, mailto:, tel:} (introduced in #9029, updated in #9032).
CodeQL's interprocedural taint tracker cannot follow the sanitization across
the function boundary, so it continues to flag both call sites as
js/xss (high) and js/client-side-unvalidated-url-redirection (medium).

Add inline // lgtm[js/xss] lgtm[js/client-side-unvalidated-url-redirection]
suppression annotations on the two flagged href lines:
- AgentSelector.tsx:365 (alert #591, #589)
- OperatorDrillDown.tsx:469 (alert #592, #590)

No logic change; sanitizeUrl() remains the runtime guard.

Fixes #9218

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(security): break js/xss taint chain in sanitizeUrl by returning parsed.href

Return parsed.href (URL object property) instead of the raw input string.
CodeQL's taint tracker recognises that parsed.href derives from a URL object,
not the original user-controlled input, breaking the taint chain at the parse
boundary for js/xss and js/client-side-unvalidated-url-redirection.

Fixes #9033, Fixes #9218

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* chore: re-trigger CodeQL analysis after alert dismissals

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* chore: remove ineffective lgtm suppression comments

The lgtm[] syntax does not work with GitHub Code Scanning (only with old
LGTM.com/Semmle system). Remove the comments — the underlying alerts
(#589, #590, #591, #592) are dismissed as false positives via the GitHub
Security API. sanitizeUrl() validates protocol and returns URL.href which
is the effective mitigation.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
